figures.caption: Figure 2. Example agentic story. Top: GEST subgraph for Marcus (red) and Sarah (blue) across two scenes. Director’s narrative: “Two old friends reunite [. . . ] Marcus grabs drinks, they chat and laugh, Marcus smokes while Sarah calls a friend, they share a hug.” Middle: Scene 1 frames. Bottom: Scene 2 frames. Post-simulation VDG: “A man answers a call [. . . ] chats with a woman [. . . ] grabs a drink [. . . ] they share a hug and a laugh.”

Table 1. Comparison with LLM-guided video generation systems. Adapted and extended from [17]. MA = multi-agent, HD = hierarchical delegation, EG = environment grounding, SR = structured representation, ET = execution target, DT = deterministic, GT = ground truth, MN = multi-actor narratives.

Figure 1. System architecture. The Director Agent explores the simulation world and plans the story, delegating individual scenes to the Scene Builder Subagent. Both agents interact through Exploration Tools (read-only) and Building Tools (constraintenforcing), which operate on the State Backend—the procedural generator repurposed as a tool layer.
